title: Store and Transmit Only Cryptographically Protected Passwords
description: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords.

checks.name: LM Password Hashing is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify LM hash storage is disabled via the Network security: Do not store LAN Manager hash value on next password change GPO setting

checks.name: Reversible Encryption is Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm Store password using reversible encryption is disabled in the local or domain password policy

checks.name: Authentication Traffic is Encrypted in Transit
checks.purpose: Verify LDAP signing and channel binding are enforced and Kerberos is used for network authentication to protect credentials in transit

checks.name: LM Password Hashing is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify LM hash storage is disabled via GPO on the server

checks.name: Reversible Encryption is Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm Store password using reversible encryption is disabled in the domain password policy

checks.name: LDAP Signing is Required
checks.purpose: Verify Domain controller: LDAP server signing requirements is set to Require signing to protect credential data in LDAP traffic

checks.name: Protected Users Security Group is Utilized for Privileged Accounts
checks.purpose: Confirm privileged accounts are members of the Protected Users security group to enforce strong credential protection (no NTLM, no RC4, no credential caching)

checks.name: Passwords are Hashed with SHA-512 or Yescrypt
checks.purpose: Verify passwords in /etc/shadow are hashed with SHA-512 ($6$) or yescrypt ($y$) and no MD5 ($1$) or DES hashes are present

checks.name: PAM is Configured to Use Strong Password Hashing
checks.purpose: Confirm pam_unix.so in /etc/pam.d/common-password uses sha512 or yescrypt hashing algorithm

checks.name: SSH Transmits Credentials Only Over Encrypted Channels
checks.purpose: Verify SSH uses strong encryption ciphers and all authentication is transmitted over an encrypted session

checks.name: Passwords are Hashed with SHA-512 or Yescrypt
checks.purpose: Verify passwords in /etc/shadow use SHA-512 ($6$) or yescrypt ($y$) hashing on Debian

checks.name: PAM is Configured to Use Strong Password Hashing
checks.purpose: Confirm pam_unix.so in /etc/pam.d/common-password uses sha512 or yescrypt on Debian

checks.name: SSH Transmits Credentials Only Over Encrypted Channels
checks.purpose: Verify SSH uses strong encryption ciphers for all authentication traffic on Debian

remediation: Disable LM hash storage via GPO. Disable reversible encryption in the password policy. Enable LDAP signing. Add privileged accounts to the Protected Users group. On Linux, configure PAM to use sha512 or yescrypt hashing and ensure no MD5 or DES hashes exist in /etc/shadow.
